title: Understanding Your Worth
description: Discover your identity and challenge limiting beliefs about yourself and technology.

content.intro: Welcome to your first step. Before learning how to code, we begin by building your belief in yourself. Your worth is inherent and cannot be diminished by cultural stereotypes or negative voices.

sections.heading: Inherent Self-Worth
sections.body: Self-worth is the belief that you are valuable and deserving of respect simply because you exist. In many Ghanaian communities, young women are taught that their worth is conditional upon marriage or household service. This is a limiting narrative. Your potential is limitless.

sections.heading: Limiting Beliefs vs. Truth
sections.body: Limiting beliefs are stories we tell ourselves that restrict our growth. Common examples include: "I am not smart enough for computer science," or "Technology is only for men." These are not facts; they are internalized fears. The truth is that capacity is built through consistent effort.

sections.heading: Rewriting Your Story
sections.body: We have the power to consciously examine and reject negative socialization. To rewrite your story, identify the negative thoughts you have about technology and replace them with empowering declarations based on effort, such as: "I can learn coding step by step."

sections.heading: Reflection Exercise
sections.body: Think about a difficult situation in your life that you successfully resolved. Write down what qualities (patience, logic, determination) you used. Keep this in mind as you start your tech path.

quiz.question: What is the definition of self-worth?
options: The amount of money you earn
The belief that you are inherently valuable and deserving
The level of education you reach
How popular you are in your neighborhood
quiz.answer: The belief that you are inherently valuable and deserving

quiz.question: Which of the following is a limiting belief?
options: I can learn HTML with practice
I am not smart enough to study computer programming
Mistakes are a normal part of the learning journey
Women can succeed in tech roles in Ghana
quiz.answer: I am not smart enough to study computer programming

quiz.question: How do you overcome a limiting belief?
options: By replacing negative thoughts with effort-focused truths
By waiting for someone else to change it
quiz.answer: By replacing negative thoughts with effort-focused truths

quiz.question: Your potential is:
options: Limitless and shaped by consistent effort
Dependent on what other people say about you
quiz.answer: Limitless and shaped by consistent effort

quiz.question: Why does Pool of Grace start with self-worth?
options: Because building confidence is key to sustained learning and growth
Because we do not teach coding
quiz.answer: Because building confidence is key to sustained learning and growth

title: Breaking Cultural Barriers
description: Explore cultural narratives about women in technology and rewrite your story.

content.intro: In this module, we examine how cultural expectations in Ghana impact women’s entry into technology and how to build internal resilience against discouragement.

sections.heading: Cultural Roles and Technology
sections.body: Societal expectations in Ghana often steer young women toward traditional roles, positioning tech as a masculine field. This socialization creates an invisible barrier, preventing girls from imagining themselves as tech creators rather than passive consumers.

sections.heading: Dealing with Active Discouragement
sections.body: Family members or friends may suggest that computer technology is "too hard" or "unsuitable for a woman." They often speak out of concern or tradition. Learn to listen politely but protect your career path using constructive boundary setting.

sections.heading: Role Model Vicarious Learning
sections.body: Seeing women succeed is one of the strongest predictors of self-belief. Ghana has incredible pioneers like Abena Asante and Ama Boateng. Their achievements prove that tech knows no gender boundaries.

sections.heading: Reflecting on Cultural Strengths
sections.body: Ghanaian culture values community support, resilience, and storytelling. We can use these positive cultural aspects to build strong networks of female developers who lift each other up.

quiz.question: What is a major cultural barrier for Ghanaian girls in tech?
options: Traditional expectations that direct women away from STEM careers
Computers not being allowed in Ghana
A lack of English language skills
quiz.answer: Traditional expectations that direct women away from STEM careers

quiz.question: When someone discourages you from tech, you should:
options: Immediately quit and find a different career
Protect your career dreams and set constructive boundaries
Agree with them that you cannot do it
quiz.answer: Protect your career dreams and set constructive boundaries

quiz.question: How do role models help build your capability?
options: They do all the coding work for you
They prove that success is possible and show you the way
They do not help at all
quiz.answer: They prove that success is possible and show you the way

quiz.question: Which of the following is an effective way to counter cultural stereotyping?
options: Connecting with female mentors and peer groups
quiz.answer: Connecting with female mentors and peer groups

quiz.question: Empowerment theory suggests that agency is:
options: The psychological capability to make choices and act on them
quiz.answer: The psychological capability to make choices and act on them

title: Building Confidence in Tech
description: Develop self-efficacy and belief in your ability to succeed in technology.

content.intro: This module introduces self-efficacy—the foundation of technical confidence—and breaks down how to build it through structured effort.

sections.heading: What is Self-Efficacy?
sections.body: Self-efficacy is your belief in your capacity to execute behaviors necessary to produce specific performance attainments. It is not general self-esteem; it is domain-specific. You build tech self-efficacy by coding.

sections.heading: The Four Sources of Self-Efficacy
sections.body: Psychologist Albert Bandura defined four sources: Mastery Experiences (hands-on coding success), Vicarious Experiences (seeing peer success), Social Persuasion (mentor encouragement), and Physiological States (anxiety control).

sections.heading: Engineering Mastery Experiences
sections.body: To build confidence, we break large tasks into tiny pieces. Solving a small syntax error is a mastery experience. Cumulative successes build absolute confidence over time.

sections.heading: Managing Learning Anxiety
sections.body: When you see red error messages on the screen, your heart rate might go up. This is a normal physiological response. Change your framing: errors are not signs of failure; they are diagnostic guides.

quiz.question: Who defined the concept of Self-Efficacy?
quiz.answer: Albert Bandura

quiz.question: Which source of self-efficacy is built when you successfully solve a programming challenge?
quiz.answer: Mastery experiences

quiz.question: If you feel highly anxious when encountering code errors, you should:
options: Frame the error as a helpful diagnostic guide instead of a failure
Restart your computer and ignore the bug
Assume you are not meant for technology
quiz.answer: Frame the error as a helpful diagnostic guide instead of a failure

quiz.question: How does social persuasion support your learning?
options: By providing encouragement and feedback from mentors
By writing the code for you
By testing your software for you
quiz.answer: By providing encouragement and feedback from mentors

quiz.question: To build technical self-efficacy, it is best to:
options: Only read books without typing code
Break tasks into tiny, manageable steps and code daily
Attempt huge, complex projects on day one
Code only when you feel 100% confident
quiz.answer: Break tasks into tiny, manageable steps and code daily

title: Overcoming Fear of Failure
description: Learn how to embrace mistakes as part of your learning journey.

content.intro: In software development, failure is not an end point; it is the core mechanism of learning. This module reframes bugs as stepping stones.

sections.heading: The Growth Mindset
sections.body: Developed by Carol Dweck, a growth mindset is the belief that basic abilities can be developed through dedication and hard work. Brains and talent are just the starting point. This view creates a love of learning.

sections.heading: Bugs are Normal
sections.body: Professional programmers spend 80% of their time fixing bugs. Writing code that fails on the first run is standard procedure. An error message is just a compiler asking for clarification.

sections.heading: Overcoming Shame of Mistakes
sections.body: Many women are socialized to feel they must be perfect to belong in academic/technical domains. We must build tolerance for being wrong and celebrate the process of debugging.

sections.heading: Ghanaian Tech Community Resilience
sections.body: In Ghana, power fluctuations or slow internet might disrupt your workflow. Building resilience means learning to adapt, save your work locally, and maintain focus.

quiz.question: What is a growth mindset?
options: The belief that intelligence is completely fixed
The belief that basic abilities can be developed through dedication and hard work
Focusing only on getting perfect grades
quiz.answer: The belief that basic abilities can be developed through dedication and hard work

quiz.question: In computer programming, bugs are:
options: A normal, everyday part of software development
quiz.answer: A normal, everyday part of software development

quiz.question: What percentage of time do professional developers spend fixing bugs and debugging?
quiz.answer: Up to 80%

quiz.question: Why do women sometimes struggle more with tech mistakes?
options: They are naturally worse at logic
They face social pressure to be perfect and fear confirming negative stereotypes
They do not care about coding
quiz.answer: They face social pressure to be perfect and fear confirming negative stereotypes

quiz.question: How should you respond to a power outage or internet cut in Ghana?
options: Adapt, use offline capabilities, save code regularly, and stay calm
Complain to your mentor without taking action
quiz.answer: Adapt, use offline capabilities, save code regularly, and stay calm

title: Your Vision and Goals
description: Create a clear vision for your technology career and set achievable goals.

content.intro: Goal setting is the bridge between aspiration and achievement. In this module, you define your tech career vision and establish SMART milestones.

sections.heading: Defining Your Career Vision
sections.body: A career vision is a clear picture of what you want your professional life to look like in the future. Are you building websites for local businesses, working at Hubtel, or starting a training NGO?

sections.heading: SMART Goal Framework
sections.body: SMART stands for Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. Instead of saying "I want to learn tech," say "I will spend 4 hours every week for the next 2 months learning JavaScript on Pool of Grace."

sections.heading: Action Plans and Habits
sections.body: Vision without execution is a hallucination. Create daily micro-habits, like coding for 30 minutes every morning before chores start.

sections.heading: Sharing Your Goals
sections.body: Research shows that sharing your goals with a supportive mentor or peer group increases your chances of success by 70%. It builds external accountability.

quiz.question: What does the S in SMART stand for?
quiz.answer: Specific

quiz.question: Which of the following is a SMART goal?
options: I want to get rich in technology
I want to study web development some day
I will code for 45 minutes every Monday and Wednesday at 8 AM for the next 6 weeks
I will try my best to learn Python
quiz.answer: I will code for 45 minutes every Monday and Wednesday at 8 AM for the next 6 weeks

quiz.question: A career vision:
options: Should be decided by your parents
Is a future picture of your professional achievements and direction
quiz.answer: Is a future picture of your professional achievements and direction

quiz.question: Why is it helpful to share your goals with your Pool of Grace mentor?
options: So they can write code for you
To build accountability and increase your chance of completion
So they can pay you a stipend
quiz.answer: To build accountability and increase your chance of completion

quiz.question: Daily micro-habits should be:
options: Done only when you feel inspired
quiz.answer: Small, realistic, and consistent actions

title: Community and Support Systems
description: Build your support network and learn how community accelerates growth.

content.intro: No programmer develops in isolation. Building a support network is critical to sustaining your momentum in Ghana’s tech ecosystem.

sections.heading: The Power of Peer Networks
sections.body: Your cohort peers on Pool of Grace are your greatest allies. Sharing frustrations, resources, and job listings builds an collective repository of knowledge.

sections.heading: Mentorship is a Catalyst
sections.body: Mentors act as navigators. They help you avoid common pitfalls, review your code, and introduce you to the professional network in Accra and Kumasi.

sections.heading: Constructive Online Forums
sections.body: Online forums like StackOverflow or the Pool of Grace community forum are tools. Learn to ask specific questions: state your goal, paste the error, and list what you tried.

sections.heading: Ghana Tech Hubs
sections.body: Familiarize yourself with community spaces like iSpace, Kumasi Hive, and Mobile Web Ghana. They provide workspace, internet, and networking meetups.

quiz.question: Why is a peer network important in tech?
options: They provide support, share learning resources, and decrease dropouts
quiz.answer: They provide support, share learning resources, and decrease dropouts

quiz.question: What is Kumasi Hive?
options: A technology hub providing workspace, community, and training
quiz.answer: A technology hub providing workspace, community, and training

quiz.question: When posting a question on the forum, you should NOT:
options: State what you are trying to accomplish
Ask someone to do the whole task for you
Show what solutions you have already tried
quiz.answer: Ask someone to do the whole task for you

quiz.question: How do mentors help accelerate your career?
options: They navigate career choices, review work, and connect you to opportunities
They attend classes in your place
quiz.answer: They navigate career choices, review work, and connect you to opportunities

quiz.question: Which of the following is a tech hub based in Accra?
quiz.answer: iSpace / Mobile Web Ghana

title: Celebrating Your Progress
description: Recognize and celebrate your achievements on your technology journey.

content.intro: This final self-worth module focuses on acknowledging your learning gains, reinforcing self-efficacy, and transitioning into technical skills training.

sections.heading: Celebrating the Process, Not Just Outcomes
sections.body: Empowerment means recognizing your progress. Did you learn what a server is? Did you write your first variables? Celebrate these moments; do not wait until you get a job.

sections.heading: Reinforcing Self-Efficacy
sections.body: Reflect on how far you have come since week one. Documenting your growth serves as a powerful reminder during future moments of self-doubt.

sections.heading: Impending Tech Transition
sections.body: You have completed the self-worth foundation! You are now psychologically armed to start coding. The technical modules will challenge you, but you are ready.

sections.heading: A Letter to Yourself
sections.body: Write down a message to yourself detailing why you started and what you want to achieve. We will show this to you when you hit a difficult coding challenge.

quiz.question: Why should you celebrate small steps in learning?
options: To reinforce self-efficacy and build positive learning loops
So you can take a long vacation
It is a waste of time
quiz.answer: To reinforce self-efficacy and build positive learning loops

quiz.question: Completing the 7 self-worth modules means you are:
options: Psychologically prepared and ready for technical coding modules
Finished with the Pool of Grace course
quiz.answer: Psychologically prepared and ready for technical coding modules

quiz.question: When you feel discouraged during technical modules, you should:
options: Assume you were wrong about your capacity
Reflect on your progress and remember your original motivation
quiz.answer: Reflect on your progress and remember your original motivation

quiz.question: What is the focus of self-worth celebration?
quiz.answer: Acknowledge effort, progress, and resilience

quiz.question: Your self-worth journey:
options: Continues as a foundation as you build technical and career resources
quiz.answer: Continues as a foundation as you build technical and career resources

title: Introduction to HTML and CSS
description: Learn the building blocks of web development from absolute scratch.

content.intro: Welcome to your first programming module. You will learn HTML to create the structure of web pages and CSS to style their design.

sections.heading: What is HTML?
sections.body:
HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language. It uses tags (like

or

) to define the headings, paragraphs, images, and links on a webpage. HTML is the skeleton.

sections.heading: What is CSS?
sections.body: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. It is the styling language used to color elements, set fonts, adjust spacing, and arrange page layouts. CSS is the clothing.

sections.heading: HTML Document Structure
sections.body: Every HTML document starts with <!DOCTYPE html>, followed by <html>, <head> (metadata and page title), and <body> (visible content).

sections.heading: Ghanaian CSS Styling Challenge
sections.body: Create a simple styled profile. Use CSS color variables matching Ghana flag colors (Red, Gold, Green, Black Star) to make a beautiful greeting card.

quiz.question: What does HTML stand for?
quiz.answer: Hyper Text Markup Language

quiz.question: Which HTML tag is used for the largest heading?
What does CSS stand for?
quiz.answer: Cascading Style Sheets

quiz.question: Which HTML element contains the visible content of a webpage?
quiz.answer: <body>

quiz.question: How do you apply a color to a text in CSS?
quiz.answer: color: blue;

title: JavaScript Fundamentals
description: Learn programming logic and JavaScript basics with hands-on exercises.

content.intro: JavaScript makes websites alive. In this module, you will learn variables, conditions, loops, and basic logic.

sections.heading: What is JavaScript?
sections.body: JavaScript (JS) is a text-based programming language used both on the client-side and server-side. It allows you to make web pages interactive. HTML is the structure, CSS is the design, JS is the behavior.

sections.heading: Variables and Data Types
sections.body: Variables are containers for storing data values. We declare variables using let, const, or var. Data types include strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, and objects.

sections.heading: Conditional Logic
sections.body: Conditional statements are used to perform different actions based on different conditions using if, else if, and else. Example: check if a student passed a quiz.

sections.heading: Loops and Functions
sections.body: Loops execute a block of code a specified number of times. Functions are reusable blocks of code that perform a specific task when invoked.

quiz.question: How do you declare a variable that cannot be reassigned?
quiz.answer: const myVar = 5;

quiz.question: Which keyword is used to create conditional logic in JavaScript?
quiz.answer: if

quiz.question: What data type represents true or false values?
quiz.answer: Boolean

quiz.question: How do you write a comment in JavaScript?
quiz.answer: // Comment

quiz.question: What is the purpose of a function?
options: To group reusable code together to perform a task
quiz.answer: To group reusable code together to perform a task

title: Building Your First Website
description: Put your HTML CSS and JavaScript skills together to build a real website.

content.intro: This module is a practical challenge. You will synthesize HTML structure, CSS design, and JavaScript behavior into a complete website.

sections.heading: Project Setup and Structure
sections.body: Learn how to organize files. Keep your index.html, style.css, and app.js clean. Use relative path links to connect stylesheet and script tags.

sections.heading: Responsive Design Principles
sections.body: A responsive website fits mobile screens, tablets, and desktops. Use CSS media queries to dynamically adjust layouts for mobile-first views.

sections.heading: DOM Manipulation
sections.body: DOM stands for Document Object Model. JavaScript can select HTML elements (e.g. document.getElementById) and dynamically update text, colors, or toggle visibility.

sections.heading: Ghanaian Small Business Showcase
sections.body: Your challenge is to build a product landing page for a local Ghanaian enterprise (e.g., shea butter, kente weaving, or farm produce) featuring an interactive order form.

quiz.question: What does DOM stand for?
quiz.answer: Document Object Model

quiz.question: How do you link an external CSS file in HTML?
quiz.answer: <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css">

quiz.question: Which CSS rule makes layouts adapt to mobile screens?
quiz.answer: @media query

quiz.question: How do you select an element by ID in JavaScript?
quiz.answer: document.getElementById("myId")

quiz.question: Where is the correct place to write metadata and stylesheet links?
quiz.answer: Inside <head>

title: Introduction to Python
description: Learn Python programming from scratch with practical Ghanaian examples.

content.intro: Python is a high-level, readable programming language used for data analysis, automation, machine learning, and backend development.

sections.heading: Why Learn Python?
sections.body: Python is known for clean, readable syntax that resembles plain English. It is a powerful tool for analyzing agricultural data, managing mobile money records, and building server logic.

sections.heading: Variables and Simple Calculations
sections.body: Python variables are declared by assignment (e.g. price = 10). Mathematical operators (+, -, *, /) can evaluate numerical equations easily.

sections.heading: Control Flow (If, For, While)
sections.body: Python uses indentation instead of curly brackets to define blocks of code. We write conditional clauses and loops using colons and indented spaces.

sections.heading: List Operations and Dictionaries
sections.body: Lists store multiple items in a single variable. Dictionaries store data in key-value pairs, which is useful for mapping data records.

quiz.question: How does Python define blocks of code?
quiz.answer: Using indentation (whitespace)

quiz.question: Which keyword defines a function in Python?
quiz.answer: def

quiz.question: What data structure stores key-value pairs in Python?
quiz.answer: Dictionary

quiz.question: How do you print text in Python?
quiz.answer: print("text")

quiz.question: Which of the following is a valid Python list?
quiz.answer: my_list = [1, 2, 3]

title: Databases and SQL
description: Understand how data is stored and learn to query databases using SQL.

content.intro: A database is an organized collection of structured information. SQL is the standard language used to communicate with databases.

sections.heading: Relational Database Concepts
sections.body: A relational database stores data in tables consisting of rows and columns. Tables relate to each other using Primary Keys and Foreign Keys.

sections.heading: Writing SELECT Queries
sections.body: SQL stands for Structured Query Language. To fetch data, we write SELECT statements: SELECT column_name FROM table_name WHERE condition.

sections.heading: CRUD Operations in SQL
sections.body: CRUD stands for Create (INSERT), Read (SELECT), Update (UPDATE), and Delete (DELETE). These four statements form the base of data management.

sections.heading: Joining Tables
sections.body: A JOIN clause combines rows from two or more tables based on a related column between them (e.g. connecting a user to their course completions).

quiz.question: What does SQL stand for?
quiz.answer: Structured Query Language

quiz.question: Which SQL statement is used to retrieve data from a database?
quiz.answer: SELECT

quiz.question: What is a Primary Key?
options: A password used to access a database
A unique identifier for each record in a table
The first column in any database table
quiz.answer: A unique identifier for each record in a table

quiz.question: Which SQL keyword is used to filter records?
quiz.answer: WHERE

quiz.question: What SQL command is used to add new rows to a table?
quiz.answer: INSERT INTO

title: Web Development Project
description: Build a complete web application project for your portfolio.

content.intro: This module is a culmination of your technology training. You will design, build, and deploy a database-driven web application.

sections.heading: Full-Stack Architecture
sections.body: A full-stack application connects a client (frontend) to a server (backend) that queries a database. You will learn how APIs (REST) bridge these systems.

sections.heading: Form Handlers and State
sections.body: Learn how to capture input from user forms in React, store it in local state, and send it to the backend via POST requests using axios.

sections.heading: Ghanaian Community Resource Directory
sections.body: Your challenge is to build a directory platform where users in Ghana can search for locally available clean water, tech centers, or community hubs.

sections.heading: Deployment Planning
sections.body: Review cloud hosting strategies. Learn about platforms like Vercel or Render for web frontend hosting and AWS for scalable backends.

quiz.question: What does a full-stack application include?
options: Both frontend and backend components including a database
quiz.answer: Both frontend and backend components including a database

quiz.question: Which library is commonly used in React to make API calls?
quiz.answer: axios

quiz.question: What is the purpose of REST APIs?
options: To allow communication between frontend client and backend server using HTTP methods
quiz.answer: To allow communication between frontend client and backend server using HTTP methods

quiz.question: What does the abbreviation URL mean?
quiz.answer: Uniform Resource Locator

quiz.question: In web development, state refers to:
options: The geographic region of the server
An object that holds component data that can change over time
quiz.answer: An object that holds component data that can change over time

title: Version Control with GitHub
description: Learn how to use GitHub to manage and share your code professionally.

content.intro: Version control is essential for modern developer collaboration. You will master Git commands and create your own GitHub profile.

sections.heading: Introduction to Git
sections.body: Git is a local version control system that tracks modifications to code files. It allows you to revert changes, view edit history, and work in branches.

sections.heading: Core Git Commands
sections.body: Learn git init, git status, git add (stage files), git commit (save local snapshots), and git push (upload changes to remote servers).

sections.heading: GitHub Collaboration
sections.body: GitHub is a cloud platform for hosting Git repositories. Learn how to open Pull Requests (PRs), request reviews, and merge changes collaboratively.

sections.heading: Creating an Open Source Profile
sections.body: Your GitHub profile is your digital CV. Learn to write a README.md file highlighting your project achievements and pin your capstone projects.

quiz.question: What is Git?
options: A local version control system that tracks file changes
quiz.answer: A local version control system that tracks file changes

quiz.question: Which command saves your staged changes in Git?
quiz.answer: git commit -m "message"

quiz.question: What is a Pull Request on GitHub?
options: A request to download a file
A proposed code change submitted for review and merge approval
quiz.answer: A proposed code change submitted for review and merge approval

quiz.question: What command uploads local commits to GitHub?
quiz.answer: git push

quiz.question: The primary branch in Git is typically named:
quiz.answer: main (or master)

title: Ghana Tech Job Market
description: Discover technology career opportunities and salary expectations in Ghana.

content.intro: This module analyzes employment trends, growth sectors, and specific opportunities in Ghana’s technology sector.

sections.heading: Sector Overview
sections.body: Ghana’s tech ecosystem is growing at 18% annually. Cities like Accra and Kumasi host hubs, telecom offices, fintech firms, and remote global employers.

sections.heading: Common Job Profiles
sections.body: Explore roles: Frontend Developer, Backend Developer, QA Engineer, Database Administrator, UX Researcher, Data Analyst, and IT Support.

sections.heading: Compensation Details
sections.body: Technology entry-level salaries in Ghana range from 30,000 to 60,000 GHS per year, significantly higher than entry level roles in other fields.

sections.heading: Empirical Context
sections.body: Research by Mensah & Agyemang (2021) shows that only 22% of developers are female. Companies are actively trying to diversify their technical engineering departments.

quiz.question: What is the average growth rate of Ghana's tech sector?
quiz.answer: 18% annually

quiz.question: Which of the following is a common entry level tech job?
quiz.answer: Frontend Developer

quiz.question: Average annual entry-level tech salaries in Ghana are around:

title: Building Your CV and Portfolio
description: Create a professional CV and portfolio that showcases your tech skills.

content.intro: A developer’s portfolio is their single best credential. You will design an online site and format a professional developer CV.

sections.heading: Structure of a Developer CV
sections.body: Highlight projects, core technical skills (languages/tools), GitHub profile link, education, and mentorship support instead of listing generic soft skills.

sections.heading: Building an Online Portfolio
sections.body: Create a responsive webpage listing your capstone projects with links to source code on GitHub and live hosted links.

sections.heading: Writing Project Summaries
sections.body: Explain what problem you solved, what technologies were used, and what your specific role was in building the feature.

sections.heading: Proofreading and Polish
sections.body: Ensure your spelling is immaculate. Use clear headings, consistent typography, and clean formatting.

quiz.question: What is the most critical link to include on a developer CV?
quiz.answer: Your GitHub profile link

quiz.question: An online portfolio should demonstrate:
options: Interactive links to projects you have built with their source code
All the books you have read
quiz.answer: Interactive links to projects you have built with their source code

quiz.question: How should you describe projects?
options: Detail the problem solved, tech used, and your implementation steps
State that the code is private
Let employers guess what it does
quiz.answer: Detail the problem solved, tech used, and your implementation steps

title: Interview Preparation
description: Prepare for technology job interviews with practice questions and techniques.

content.intro: This module covers interviewing strategies: coding whiteboard tests, live challenges, and behavioral questions.

sections.heading: The Tech Interview Process
sections.body: Interviews usually have three parts: Initial screening call, Technical evaluation (coding task or code walkthrough), and Behavioral fit check.

sections.heading: Handling Coding Exercises
sections.body: When asked to write code, always speak your thought process out loud. Clarify constraints before typing. Focus on a working solution first, then optimize.

sections.heading: The STAR Method for Behavioral Questions
sections.body: STAR stands for Situation, Task, Action, and Result. Use this framework to describe how you solved technical bugs or worked in teams.

sections.heading: Asking Questions at the End
sections.body: Show interest by asking: "What tech stack does the team use?" or "What are the goals for this position in the first 6 months?"

quiz.question: What does STAR stand for in behavioral interviews?
quiz.answer: Situation, Task, Action, Result

quiz.question: If you get stuck during a live coding interview, you should:
options: Quietly stop typing and wait for it to end
Explain your thought process out loud and ask clarifying questions
quiz.answer: Explain your thought process out loud and ask clarifying questions

quiz.question: What is a good question to ask the interviewer?
options: When do I get a raise?
What technologies does the engineering team use and what are the main goals?
quiz.answer: What technologies does the engineering team use and what are the main goals?

title: Networking and LinkedIn
description: Build your professional network and create a strong LinkedIn presence.

content.intro: Networking accounts for over 70% of tech hires in Ghana. You will learn to build a LinkedIn profile and engage in the local ecosystem.

sections.heading: Your LinkedIn Profile
sections.body: A developer profile needs: a clear headshot, a headline stating your technical focus (e.g. Frontend Developer | React), and a summary of your path.

sections.heading: Connecting with Intention
sections.body: Do not send spam connection requests. Write short personalized notes introducing yourself, your work on Pool of Grace, and your learning goals.

sections.heading: Tech Communities in Ghana
sections.body: Join local Slack directories, WhatsApp groups, and mailing lists like DevCongress and Women in Tech Africa networks.

sections.heading: Attending Meetups
sections.body: Attend local tech events. Introduce yourself to speakers after the talk. Exchange GitHub links instead of traditional business cards.

quiz.question: What percentage of tech jobs are filled through networking?
quiz.answer: Over 70%

quiz.question: What is a strong LinkedIn headline for a graduate of this platform?
options: Frontend Developer | React & Python | Pool of Grace Graduate
quiz.answer: Frontend Developer | React & Python | Pool of Grace Graduate

quiz.question: How should you approach local tech meetups?
options: Stand in a corner and check your phone
Attend, listen to talks, and exchange GitHub links with peers and speakers
quiz.answer: Attend, listen to talks, and exchange GitHub links with peers and speakers

title: Freelancing and Entrepreneurship
description: Explore freelancing opportunities and how to start your own tech business in Ghana.

content.intro: Freelancing offers geographical flexibility. This module covers platforms, client management, pricing, and local startup steps.

sections.heading: Freelancing Platforms
sections.body: Create accounts on Upwork, Fiverr, or Freelancer. Set up portfolios showing web design templates, SQL reports, or script automation.

sections.heading: Setting Your Prices
sections.body: Understand hourly vs fixed pricing. Calculate your base costs (electricity, internet, taxes) to establish a minimum profitable rate.

sections.heading: Managing Client Projects
sections.body: Write contracts detailing project milestones, timelines, and payment stages. Avoid scope creep by listing exactly what is included in the cost.

sections.heading: Registering a Business in Ghana
sections.body: To register, visit the Registrar General’s Department. Establish a sole proprietorship or limited liability company to invoice clients.

quiz.question: Which of the following is a popular freelancing platform?
quiz.answer: Upwork

quiz.question: To avoid scope creep, a freelancer should:
options: Do all extra work for free
Define project milestones and boundaries clearly in a written contract
quiz.answer: Define project milestones and boundaries clearly in a written contract

quiz.question: Where do you register a local business in Ghana?
quiz.answer: The Registrar General's Department

title: Continuing Your Tech Journey
description: Plan your next steps and discover advanced learning pathways in technology.

content.intro: Technology evolves daily. This final module helps you build a lifelong learning system to stay competitive in the industry.

sections.heading: Lifelong Learning in Tech
sections.body: As a developer, your learning never stops. Commit to studying new libraries, frameworks, or architecture concepts for a few hours every week.

sections.heading: Choosing a Specialization
sections.body: After mastering the basics, specialize in: Frontend (React, Next.js), Backend (Node, Django), Data Science, Mobile Apps, or Cloud Engineering.

sections.heading: Contributing to Open Source
sections.body: Contributing to open source repositories on GitHub is an excellent way to practice teamwork, code quality, and gain global visibility.

sections.heading: Staying Connected to Pool of Grace
sections.body: Join our alumni directory. Transition from a participant to a peer mentor, helping the next cohort of young women start their tech path.

quiz.question: Why is continuous learning important in technology?
options: Because languages and frameworks change constantly
quiz.answer: Because languages and frameworks change constantly

quiz.question: What is a good next step after completing these modules?
options: Choose a technical specialization (like React or Django) and build complex projects
Wait for a company to train you from scratch
quiz.answer: Choose a technical specialization (like React or Django) and build complex projects

quiz.question: How can you give back to Pool of Grace as an alumna?
options: By becoming a peer mentor and joining the alumni directory to support new cohorts
quiz.answer: By becoming a peer mentor and joining the alumni directory to support new cohorts
